en.extract:
The plain-pouched hornbill (Rhyticeros subruficollis) is a species of hornbill in the family Bucerotidae. It is found in forests of the Dawna Range and the Tenasserim Hills of southern Myanmar, adjacent parts of western Thailand and northern Peninsular Malaysia.
It is threatened by habitat loss.
The Oriental pied Hornbill is a nest competitor of the plain-pouched hornbill. They primarily nest in tetrameles and fig trees.
It eats fruit, including mahogany and mulberries, and insects, especially cicadas and beetles.


References

Birdlife Species Factsheet.

The '''plain-pouched hornbill''' ('''''Rhyticeros subruficollis''''') is a species of [[hornbill]] in the family [[Bucerotidae]]. It is found in forests of the [[Dawna Range]] and the [[Tenasserim Hills]] of southern [[Myanmar]], adjacent parts of western [[Thailand]] and northern [[Peninsular Malaysia]].

It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

The Oriental pied Hornbill is a nest competitor of the plain-pouched hornbill.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Aerts |first=Raf |last2=Volkaert |first2=Hugo |last3=Roongruangsree |first3=Nalinee |last4=Roongruangsree |first4=U-Tai |last5=Swennen |first5=Rony |last6=Muys |first6=Bart |date=December 2009 |title=Site requirements of the endangered rosewood Dalbergia oliveri in a tropical deciduous forest in northern Thailand |journal=Forest Ecology and Management |volume=259 |issue=1 |pages=117–123 |doi=10.1016/j.foreco.2009.09.051 |issn=0378-1127}}</ref> They primarily nest in [[tetrameles]] and [[Ficus|fig trees]].<ref name=":0" />

It eats fruit, including [[mahogany]] and [[mulberries]], and insects, especially cicadas and beetles.<ref name=":0" />
